  "findings": [
    "Milkshake oil cap indicates coolant contamination in engine oil",
    "Emulsified oil residue on filler cap"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work and quarantine vehicle/engine",
    "Inspect for head gasket failure, cracked block, or oil cooler leak",
    "Drain and flush contaminated oil and coolant systems",
    "Replace defective components per OEM specs"
  ],
  "rationale": "Milkshake appearance on oil cap is a classic sign of coolant mixing with engine oil, typically due to a failed head gasket, cracked engine block, or leaking oil cooler. This condition compromises lubrication and can lead to catastrophic engine failure. The component is installed-defective and must be corrected before proceeding.",
  "references": ["OEM specs for engine assembly and fluid integrity"]
}
